steps.title: Read the passage for the key constraint
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'depleted': The passage says bees leave patches of low species richness, not patches that are emptied of resources. “Depleted” targets quantity, not variety. ✗
  • Choice B — 'homogeneous': “Homogeneous” means all the same type. If a dense patch has flowers of only one species, it has low species richness, which is exactly the condition the passage describes. ✓
  • Choice C — 'immature': Nothing in the passage links flower maturity to species richness or to bees’ decision to forage elsewhere. ✗
  • Choice D — 'dispersed': The passage describes dense patches, so “dispersed” (widely scattered) contradicts the premise. ✗

steps.title: Confirm with the passage logic
steps.content:
Bees abandon dense patches when those patches have “low species richness” — meaning every flower is essentially the same type. That is the definition of homogeneous. No other choice fits the species-richness language in the passage.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
